game_name: Master of Orion II: Battle at Antares

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: foundational design phase; 13 pre-defined races or custom via 10-point pick system
strategic choice affecting research, wealth, espionage vulnerability, assimilation rates; upgradeable via research
multi-planet systems with varying desirability; resource management (farming/industry/research balance)
occupy desirable star systems; challenging when fleets are small and low-tech
colonies require food surplus or starve; colonist allocation between farming, industry, research
ship direction changes, marine boarding mechanics, technology-dependent options
government-dependent vulnerability; democracy most vulnerable, unification most resistant
requires advanced technology; game mechanic not available in original Master of Orion
grants Avenger starship and non-researchable Antaran technologies; significant strategic advantage but not auto-win
escaped from pocket dimension; increasingly powerful fleets assault colonies; escalating tension
one of three victory conditions; player must assault Antaran homeworld through portal

typical_tension_triggers: space monster encounters in desirable systems
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based asynch progression with escalating threat narrative; early game resource management and exploration, mid-game competition and espionage, late-game existential Antaran threat

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: desirable star systems guarded by space monsters (risk-reward signal)
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Orion star system contains Guardian warship and hidden Antaran technologies (prestige location)

difficulty_curve.early_game: manageable; small fleets, low-tech, focus on colonization and basic economy; space monsters present moderate challenge
difficulty_curve.mid_game: increasing complexity; opponent competition, espionage threats, tactical combat depth, technology branching
difficulty_curve.late_game: escalating threat; Antaran invasions with increasingly powerful fleets; multiple victory paths create strategic tension
notable_spikes: First space monster encounter in desirable system (early game difficulty check)
Antaran emergence and initial invasion waves (transition to late game threat)
Late-game Antaran fleet power scaling (requires sustained military or diplomatic progress)

notable_level_patterns.description: Players discover star systems, encounter guardians, build fleets to defend/conquer, then manage conquered territory resources
Three distinct victory conditions (military conquest, diplomatic election 2/3 majority, Antaran assault) create different pacing and strategy paths
Late-game progression defined by rising Antaran threat; requires player to choose between military preparation vs. pursuing other victory conditions
Design space created by combining 13 races × 5 governments × custom points system; early choices lock in long-term economic/military profiles
